---
title: "Trade Log"
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from analysis.breakout_strategy import run_analysis
from analysis.filtered_backtest import run_filtered_backtest
from IPython.display import HTML, display

analysis = run_analysis()
ml_overlay = run_filtered_backtest(filter_mode="scaled_selected_asset")
display(analysis["blotter_display"])
```

## Downloads

```{python}
export_paths = analysis["export_paths"]

HTML(
    f'''
    <p><a href="{export_paths["blotter_csv_href"]}" download>Download baseline trade blotter CSV</a></p>
    <p><a href="{export_paths["ledger_csv_href"]}" download>Download baseline trade ledger CSV</a></p>
    <p><a href="downloads/scaled_selected_asset_backtest_comparison.csv" download>Download ML overlay comparison CSV</a></p>
    <p><a href="downloads/scaled_selected_asset_backtest_summary.json" download>Download ML overlay summary JSON</a></p>
    '''
)
```

## Interactive Trade Return View

```{python}
import plotly.express as px

blotter = analysis["blotter"]

fig = px.scatter(
    blotter,
    x="entry_timestamp",
    y="trade_return",
    size="qty",
    color="trade_outcome",
    hover_data=["exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "pnl", "holding_periods", "exit_reason"],
    title="Baseline Breakout Trade Returns",
    labels={"entry_timestamp": "Entry Date", "trade_return": "Trade Return"},
)
fig.update_layout(height=460)
fig.show()
```

## Best And Worst Trades

```{python}
best_trade = blotter.loc[:, ["entry_timestamp", "exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "trade_return", "pnl", "exit_reason"]].sort_values("trade_return", ascending=False).head(3)
worst_trade = blotter.loc[:, ["entry_timestamp", "exit_timestamp", "entry_price", "exit_price", "trade_return", "pnl", "exit_reason"]].sort_values("trade_return", ascending=True).head(3)

print("Best trades")
display(best_trade)

print("Worst trades")
display(worst_trade)
```

## Ledger Preview

```{python}
display(analysis["ledger_display"])
```

## ML Overlay Comparison

```{python}
display(ml_overlay["comparison"])
```

The final ML overlay keeps the same trade timestamps as the baseline and changes capital allocation rather than deleting all low-score trades. That is why the overlay comparison is best read as a portfolio-allocation enhancement, not as a second independent blotter.

## Why The Trade Log Matters

The trade log makes the backtest auditable. It shows:

- when entries and exits occurred
- how long trades stayed open
- whether exits came from success, stop-loss, timeout, or breakout failure
- how profits and losses were distributed across trades
- how the final ML overlay changes position sizing on top of those same breakout opportunities
